page: Microsoft Azure EC32es_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ECEV6 server family. Powered by an x86_64 Intel processor, this instance features 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The underlying virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server configuration does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external volumes. The instance is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance persistent disks to handle disk-bound operations. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the high memory density of 8.0 GB per vCPU and the dedicated compute allocation, the EC32es_v6 is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for enterprise applications requiring large memory footprints without the overhead of local physical disks. Suitable workloads include relational databases, in-memory caching layers, and enterprise resource planning systems.
description: Microsoft Azure EC32es_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ECEV6 family, built on the x86_64 Intel processor architecture and managed by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to high-performance external storage volumes. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this server. The high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation make this instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive enterprise applications. It is well-suited for workloads such as relational databases, in-memory data stores, and large-scale enterprise application servers.
